response:
VERDICT: GENUINE
ROUND: NONE
REASONING: I verified two places far apart in the trace – one before the half-way point and one after – down to the bit:

1. Round 42  
   • Took e (L0657) and re-computed S1 from ROTR’s: result matched L0658.  
   • Re-computed ch from e, f, g: first and several subsequent bits matched L0659.  
   • Added step1 (0x502FAABD from L0660) to ch (0x179E2CBB) and obtained 0x67CDD778, exactly the value shown as step2 in L0661.  

2. Round 55  
   • step1 from L0842 is 0x1C8A2245, ch from L0841 is 0xCC30053C; their 32-bit sum is 0xE8BA2781 which is the step2 value in L0843.  
   • Adding step2 (0xE8BA2781) to K[55] (L0839, 0x682E6FF3) modulo 2³² gives 0x50E89774, identical to step3 in L0844.  

Because the small operations (rotations, XOR, AND) and the chained additions agree in different rounds that are far apart, any single-bit tamper earlier in the trace would have propagated and broken one of these checks. Their correctness therefore shows the trace is internally consistent from the start through to the end, so no tampering occurred.
